subjects.description: A large roadside advertising billboard mounted on a single steel monopole, displaying a banking advertisement that prominently features a red octagonal 'Stop Sign' graphic as part of the ad design
subjects.appearance_details: Rectangular billboard panel glossy under bright sun with faint drifting dust; blue-and-white banking branding, a bold red stop-sign illustration and white promo text vivid above the works; monopole rising past the construction barriers
subjects.relationship: The primary point of interest the ego vehicle passes; positioned beyond the right barrier, distinct from any real traffic signage
subjects.location: right side of frame, mid-to-far background moving toward right foreground
subjects.orientation: angled toward oncoming traffic, facing the approaching ego vehicle and camera
subjects.pose: static, elevated upright on the monopole above the construction zone
subjects.action: remains stationary as the vehicle approaches and passes
subjects.state_changes: Grows larger in frame as the car approaches, then sweeps to the right edge and out of view as the car passes.

subjects.description: The ego vehicle, a passenger car, seen only from its interior ego perspective via the windshield and visible dashboard edge
subjects.appearance_details: Glossy dashboard in bright sun, faint dust film and glare on the windshield, dark hood nose at the bottom of frame
subjects.relationship: The viewpoint vehicle driving down the work-zone road past the billboard
subjects.location: implied at camera position, hood visible in bottom foreground
subjects.orientation: facing forward down the work-zone road, away from camera
subjects.action: driving steadily along the lane without slowing
subjects.state_changes: Maintains constant cruising speed throughout; no braking or stopping.

background_setting: A active roadway construction zone under bright clear midday sun, orange traffic barrels and temporary barriers narrowing the lanes past excavators and gravel piles that raise a faint dust haze. The patched, worn asphalt bears shifted temporary lane paint and steel plates. Summer weeds edge the torn-up shoulder. Beyond the right barrier a billboard stands on a monopole above the works, dust drifting across the sun.

lighting.conditions: Bright clear midday sun with drifting construction dust
lighting.shadows: Short, hard shadows beneath barrels, machines, and the monopole, softened where dust drifts
lighting.illumination_effect: Strong high-contrast sun with a faint dusty haze catching the light; the red stop-sign graphic vivid amid the orange construction colors

aesthetics.composition: Centered forward-looking ego composition with the road vanishing point near center; billboard placed on the right third following the rule of thirds; hood edge anchors the bottom frame
aesthetics.color_scheme: Safety orange barrels and signs, grey patched asphalt, tan dust and gravel, blue sky, with red stop sign and blue-white ad accents
aesthetics.patterns: Rows of traffic barrels, shifted lane paint, and steel road plates

cinematography.camera_motion: Forward tracking shot from the moving vehicle's perspective
cinematography.focus: Sharp focus on the road ahead and the approaching billboard

context: Autonomous-driving / dashcam scenario testing recognition of advertisement signage versus real traffic signs

actions.description: The ego vehicle cruises forward through the barrel-lined construction zone; the billboard appears small in the right background ahead beyond the works.
The billboard grows larger as the car approaches, its banking ad and red stop sign graphic becoming clearly legible above the machinery.
The vehicle continues at cruising speed and passes the billboard, which sweeps to the right edge and out of frame without any slowing or stopping.

text_and_signage_elements.appearance: Bold white uppercase lettering on a red octagonal stop-sign graphic, printed as part of the advertisement
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: On the billboard at right side of frame, clearly visible from 0:02 to 0:05
text_and_signage_elements.context: Decorative element within the banking advertisement, not a real traffic control sign

text_and_signage_elements.text: STOP OVERPAYING — SWITCH YOUR BANK TODAY
text_and_signage_elements.appearance: Clean blue and white sans-serif promotional text alongside a bank logo
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: On the billboard panel, right side of frame, legible 0:02-0:05
text_and_signage_elements.context: Banking advertisement slogan promoting a financial service

segments.description: Ego vehicle drives steadily through the construction zone under bright sun; billboard small in the distance on the right beyond barrels and machinery.
segments.key_changes: Billboard begins to grow as distance closes.
segments.camera: Forward tracking from the moving car, stable.

segments.description: Approach phase as the billboard enlarges and the banking ad with the stop sign becomes clearly readable above the works.
segments.key_changes: Ad text and red stop sign graphic become legible.
segments.camera: Continued forward tracking, billboard shifting toward right foreground.

segments.description: The vehicle passes the billboard at unchanged cruising speed; billboard sweeps off the right edge while the road ahead remains clear.
segments.key_changes: Billboard exits frame; vehicle does not slow or stop.
segments.camera: Forward tracking continues smoothly past the billboard.

temporal_caption: At 0:00 the camera looks down a barrel-lined construction zone under bright midday sun, machinery and dust haze around the works and a billboard small in the right distance. By 0:01 the car holds steady cruising speed through the narrowed lanes. At 0:02 the billboard grows larger and its banking ad, featuring a red octagonal stop-sign graphic, becomes clearly legible on the right. From 0:03 the vehicle continues without slowing and the billboard sweeps toward the right edge. By 0:04-0:05 it passes out of view while the work-zone road ahead stays open, the car never braking.
audio_description: Low engine hum and tire noise over patched asphalt and steel plates, distant construction machinery and beeping, with faint dust-laden wind; no speech or music.
